title: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
subhead: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Danish leader reflects on Donald Trump's statements at the NATO summit in Ankara regarding a potential US takeover of the island, as well as on the strategic autonomy for Europe that she is calling for.

title: French parliamentary inquiry slams government's handling of British border
subhead: The report by a Communist MP denounces France's security-first approach as ineffective, dangerous and lacking democratic oversight.

title: Tour de France: Kooij wins 5th stage in chaotic sprint finish
subhead: Olav Kooij, a 24-year-old Dutch debutant Tour contender, beat German Max Kanter into second, with Belgian Tim Merlier coming in third place for the Tour de France's fifth stage, after several riders crashed and others were slowed.

title: 2026 World Cup: Recent FIFA decisions ramp up pressure on referees
subhead: From the Balogun case to the appointment of an all-Argentinian refereeing team for the France-Morocco match on Thursday, FIFA itself has shaken the refereeing corps, undermining their authority and fueling suspicions of bias.

title: Philosopher Mélanie Plouviez: 'The rich are not rich alone; they are in a social world that protects them'
subhead: Wealth is not determined solely by the possessions and assets one accumulates, philosopher Mélanie Plouviez explains in an interview with Le Monde. Since antiquity, a person's fortune has also been defined by one's social standing, relationships of dominance, dependence and honor.
